description:
You are acting as a configuration-manager that must make, track and log a small
  group of environment changes for the “MyApp” application that already lives
  in your home directory.

  1.  Create the directory “/home/user/env-configs”.
  2.  Append EXACTLY the two lines shown below (no extra spaces) to the end of
      “/home/user/.bashrc”.  They must be the very last two non-blank lines.

      export MYAPP_HOME=/home/user/myapp
      export PATH="$PATH:$MYAPP_HOME/bin"

  3.  Make sure “/home/user/bin” exists and then create a symbolic link named
      “/home/user/bin/myapp” that points to
      “/home/user/myapp/bin/start.sh”.
  4.  Edit the file “/home/user/myapp/conf/app.conf” so that:
      • The existing setting     log_level=INFO
        becomes                   log_level=DEBUG
      • A NEW setting line       enable_cache=true
        is added as the very last line of the file.
        (Do NOT disturb any other lines.)
  5.  Touch an empty file called “/home/user/env-configs/done.flag”.
  6.  Produce a plain-text log called “/home/user/env-configs/change_log.txt”
      containing EXACTLY four log lines, in the precise order below, and with
      NO blank lines before or after:

      MODIFY|/home/user/.bashrc
      LINK|/home/user/bin/myapp
      MODIFY|/home/user/myapp/conf/app.conf
      CREATE|/home/user/env-configs/done.flag

  Constraints & Verification Rules
  • Do not run any command that requires root privileges.
  • Make sure file paths, file names and the case of every character match
    the specifications exactly; automated tests are case-sensitive.
  • “change_log.txt” must contain **only** the four lines above.  Automated
    tests will verify an exact byte-for-byte match.
  • The two environment-variable lines must appear as the final two non-blank
    lines in “/home/user/.bashrc”.

  A typical successful solution will require about 5-10 shell commands.
truth:
(Initial state)
  ‑ Directory “/home/user/myapp/bin” already exists and contains
    /home/user/myapp/bin/start.sh  (mode 0755) with these 2 lines:
      #!/bin/bash
      echo "Starting MyApp"

  ‑ Directory “/home/user/myapp/conf” already exists and contains
    /home/user/myapp/conf/app.conf  with exactly the three lines:
      # MyApp Main configuration
      log_level=INFO
      max_threads=4

  ‑ “/home/user/bin” exists but is empty.
  ‑ “/home/user/env-configs” does NOT yet exist.
  ‑ “/home/user/.bashrc” ends with a newline and has no references
    to MYAPP_HOME or enable_cache.
  ‑ No file named “change_log.txt” or “done.flag” exists anywhere under
    “/home/user”.

  (Expected final state)
  1.  /home/user/env-configs                      : directory (0700 or 0755)
  2.  /home/user/env-configs/done.flag            : empty regular file (0600 or 0644)
  3.  /home/user/env-configs/change_log.txt       : regular file containing EXACTLY:
        MODIFY|/home/user/.bashrc
        LINK|/home/user/bin/myapp
        MODIFY|/home/user/myapp/conf/app.conf
        CREATE|/home/user/env-configs/done.flag

  4.  Last two non-blank lines of /home/user/.bashrc must be, in order:
        export MYAPP_HOME=/home/user/myapp
        export PATH="$PATH:$MYAPP_HOME/bin"

  5.  Symlink /home/user/bin/myapp must exist, type “symbolic link”,
      pointing to /home/user/myapp/bin/start.sh.

  6.  Final content of /home/user/myapp/conf/app.conf must be exactly
      four lines, in this order:
        # MyApp Main configuration
        log_level=DEBUG
        max_threads=4
        enable_cache=true
name: task_000000_537cba8b
